title: iam-jit diagnostics bundle manifest (cross-product)
description:
The 09-manifest.json file inside every `*bounce diagnostics bundle` ZIP. Pinned cross-product shape so a single triage tool (or LLM-assisted support response) can parse a diagnostics bundle from any Bounce product identically.

The ZIP itself contains a fixed set of named sections (00-readme.txt, 01-config.yaml, 02-version.txt, 03-startup-banner.txt, 04-recent-decisions.jsonl, 05-healthz.json, 06-system.txt, 07-listener.json, 08-panics.txt, 09-manifest.json). 09-manifest.json describes the other 8 + carries provenance + the redaction marker convention. Authoritative builder: src/iam_jit/bouncer/diagnostics.py (ibounce); internal/cli/diagnostics.go (kbounce, dbounce, gbounce — same shape per [[cross-product-agent-parity]]).

The manifest is the source of truth for what's IN the bundle — a triage tool MUST iterate `entries[]` rather than hardcoding filenames so the format can grow additively across versions.

bundle_version.description: Schema-version of this manifest. Pre-#288 bundles emit int 1; future bumps are integer. Triage tools MUST tolerate higher values (additive evolution).

format.description: Per-product format magic. Constrained so a tool that grepped for `format == "ibounce.diagnostics"` keeps working.

product.description: Per-product name. Mirrors metadata.product.name in the OCSF audit-event shape.

ibounce_version.description: Present in ibounce bundles only. Other products use the matching per-product version field (kbounce_version, dbounce_version, gbounce_version).

generated_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp the bundle was assembled. The exact format the diagnostics builder emits is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

redaction_marker.description: The literal string substituted for any redacted secret in the bundle's text sections. Constant across the suite so a triage tool can grep for it uniformly.

user_id_hash_format.description: Description of how user IDs were hashed before landing in the bundle. Shape: `<prefix><N-hex>` (e.g. `usr_<12hex>`).

entries.description: Per-file inventory. The triage tool MUST iterate this list rather than hardcoding filenames.

sha256.description: Hex sha256 of the section's bytes. Stable across re-zips of the same content; used by support to confirm a customer's bundle hasn't been hand-edited.
